provenance.source_corpus: White spruce enrichment planting in boreal mixedwoods as influenced by localized site preparation: 11-year update

source_spans.text: Localized site preparation did not significantly affect growth or survival for white spruce compared to control conditions.

nodes.name: growth or survival for white spruce

source_spans.text: Localized site preparation did not significantly affect growth or survival for white spruce compared to control conditions.

nodes.name: proportion of white and black spruce

source_spans.text: Furthermore, localized site preparation did not increase the proportion of white and black spruce, as evaluated by basal area.
Our results suggest that white spruce can be successfully established in enrichment planting in fir-dominated boreal forests, without site preparation.
Our results suggest that white spruce can be successfully established in enrichment planting in fir-dominated boreal forests, without site preparation.
Spruce basal area was 0.02 m²/ha higher in plots planted with white spruce compared to plots planted with black spruce
Spruce basal area was 0.02 m²/ha higher in plots planted with white spruce compared to plots planted with black spruce

nodes.name: seedlings planted with the root collar at the organic-mineral interface

source_spans.text: However, long-term benefits of this practice are not necessarily observed (Paquette et al. 2011), as was the case in our study.
